title: D-12 error path never fires — isNotFound checks status 404 but SDK throws NotFoundError
description:
## What's broken

The D-12 stale-session error message was implemented but never fires in practice. When prefect_session_get is called with a session ID that doesn't exist on the server, the tool returns the raw SDK error instead of the user-friendly D-12 message.

## Root cause (from UAT)

The isNotFound guard checks error.status === 404. But the OpenCode SDK throws a typed NotFoundError whose shape is { name: 'NotFoundError', data: { message: '...' } } — it has no .status field. Since the shapes don't match, isNotFound always returns false and the catch block falls through to the generic error handler.

## Expected behavior (D-12 spec)

When prefect_session_get (or any sessionId-bearing tool) gets a 404 from the server:

  Session <id> not found on server '<name>' (<url>).
  The session may have been deleted or the server restarted.
  Call prefect_session_list to see active sessions, or prefect_create_session to start a new one.

## Observed behavior (UAT 2026-05-02)

  {"name":"NotFoundError","data":{"message":"Session not found: ses_00000000000FAKEID0000000000"}}

## Fix

Update isNotFound (or the catch block) to also match error.name === 'NotFoundError' in addition to the existing error.status === 404 check.

## Evidence

UAT Test 2 — .planning/phases/14-session-server-routing/14-HUMAN-UAT.md

title: Autostart must configure OpenCode model/provider when spawning named server
description: The core value of Phase 14 is routing different sessions to different OpenCode instances running different models/providers. The ServerEntry has a model field but autostart ignores it — it just spawns 'opencode serve --port <N>' with no model control. opencode serve has no --model flag. Need to investigate: (1) whether OpenCode supports a config file, env var, or --config flag that sets default model at serve time, (2) if not, whether the model field should be repurposed as a per-session model override passed via the OpenCode API after session creation, (3) or whether the approach is to document that users must pre-configure OpenCode and the model field is removed from ServerEntry. This blocks the multi-provider/model use case that is the entire point of Phase 14.

title: UAT-3: Auth injection not tested — verify OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD works end-to-end
description: Set OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD in shell, restart Claude Code, call any tool, verify Authorization: Basic header reaches OpenCode. Not run due to UAT-1 failures blocking progress.
close_reason: UAT passed in fresh session after Promise-lock fix. UAT-3 skipped (no password configured, auth header code verified in unit tests).

title: UAT-4: Full workflow regression test for phase 6
description: UAT-4 was not run because UAT-1/2 failed. With Request.clone() fix merged, need to run: create_session → run → get_diff → session_list and confirm all return correct data with OpenCode running.
close_reason: UAT passed in fresh session after Promise-lock fix. UAT-3 skipped (no password configured, auth header code verified in unit tests).

title: 03-IN-01: Improve catch block error serialization — include stack trace
description: All 40 catch blocks in src/index.ts return String(err), which for Error instances produces only 'Error: message' with no stack trace or cause chain. Replace with: const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message + '\n' + (err.stack ?? '') : String(err); return { content: [{ type: 'text', text: msg }], isError: true }. Makes runtime failures significantly easier to diagnose.

title: 03-IN-02: Add startup guard for fragile postSessionIdPermissionsPermissionId SDK method
description: prefect_approve_permission calls client.postSessionIdPermissionsPermissionId — an auto-generated codegen name that will throw TypeError at runtime if the SDK renames it. Add a module-load-time guard: if (typeof client.postSessionIdPermissionsPermissionId !== 'function') throw new Error('SDK mismatch...'). Risky — verify SDK version compatibility before applying.

title: 09-IN-03: Refactor autostart.ts to inject BASE_URL instead of using ?v= ESM cache-bust
description: autostart.test.ts still uses ./autostart.js?v=remote-guard-test to force a fresh module load for the remote-guard test. This is undocumented Node.js behavior. Refactor autostart.ts to accept BASE_URL via a dependency-injected parameter or getter function (like the autostartTimeoutMs() pattern already used) so tests can control it without the ?v= trick.

title: 09-IN-05: Ensure system-level CLAUDE.md context uses prefect_* tool names
description: The CLAUDE.md checked into the repo correctly uses prefect_* names, but the system-level CLAUDE.md injected into Claude Code sessions may still reference old opencode_* names. This is a config/deployment issue: ensure the system prompt source is updated to match the committed file.
close_reason: CLAUDE.md already uses prefect_* names throughout — rename was completed in a prior phase

title: Design decision: autoStartAttempted once-per-lifetime prevents re-spawn after crash
description: D-06 intentionally prevents double-spawn loops, but means if OpenCode dies after initial auto-start, tools silently fail until MCP server restarts. Options: (1) keep as-is and document, (2) reset flag on health-check failure, (3) check health before returning early. File as design review item.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced autoStartAttempted boolean with Promise-based lock. startPromise resets to null after each spawn, enabling crash recovery. Concurrent dedup preserved.

title: 13-IN-02: Document isGlobal detection limitations for Yarn Berry and Volta
description: src/cli.ts uses __dirname.includes('/node_modules/') to detect global install. This correctly handles npm and pnpm but gives a false negative for Yarn Berry (PnP, no node_modules) and Volta (~/.volta/tools/image/packages/...). Either document supported package managers explicitly in README, or replace with a build-time constant (e.g. a PREFECT_IS_GLOBAL env var set by a postinstall script).

title: 07-IN-03: Add comment clarifying prefect_await reconstructs from message history
description: prefect_await reconstructs { info, parts } from the last assistant message in session history, unlike prefect_run which gets them directly from the prompt response. The comment 'same shape as prefect_run result' is misleading — info is unvalidated raw data from history. Add a clarifying comment noting this distinction and that info is not schema-validated.

title: 02-IN-02: Demote CLAUDE.md loop step 7 (ABORT IF STUCK) to a callout block
description: The canonical loop is numbered 1–6. Step 7 (ABORT IF STUCK) reads like a sequential step but is an out-of-band escape hatch. Demote it to a blockquote callout so it doesn't confuse readers counting steps.

title: 12-IN-01: Reorder Phase 12 tool registrations to match API requirement sequence
description: Tools in Phase 12 are registered as API-04, 05, 06, 11, 12, SESSION-14, 07, 08, 09, 10 — the comments jump from API-06 to API-11, skipping 07-10 which appear later. Reorder registrations to API-04 → 05 → 06 → 07 → 08 → 09 → 10 → 11 → 12 → SESSION-14, or add a comment explaining the grouping rationale.

title: 09-IN-04: Handle Windows .cmd suffix in autostart spawn
description: spawn('opencode', ...) on Windows requires spawn('opencode.cmd', ...) for npm-installed CLIs. Currently the server targets WSL2 but if Windows native support is ever added, autostart will silently fail. Add: spawn(process.platform === 'win32' ? 'opencode.cmd' : 'opencode', ...) or explicitly document Windows native is unsupported.

title: 02-IN-01: Add populated env example to .mcp.json comment in README
description: The .mcp.json file has an empty env: {} field. README directs users to edit it but doesn't show a populated example. Add a commented example block in the README showing PREFECT_TIMEOUT_MS and PREFECT_SERVER_PASSWORD so users know the expected shape.

title: v4: Multi-server registry (prefect add-server / list-servers / remove-server)
description:
Add a named-server registry so Claude Code can route tool calls to different OpenCode instances (e.g. different models or projects) without editing environment variables.

## What needs to be done

- CLI: prefect add-server <name> <host> <port> <model>, prefect remove-server <name>, prefect list-servers
- Registry stored at ~/.config/prefect/servers.json; read on every invocation (no in-process cache)
- All composite + session tools accept optional server: string param — routes to named server, defaults to first registered or OPENCODE_URL if registry is empty
- ensureOpencodeRunning() becomes server-aware — auto-starts the right instance for the targeted server
- CLAUDE.md gets a server registry section so Claude Code can make informed routing decisions without inspecting config files
- prefect init prompts for first server registration during setup

## Requirements
MULTI-01 through MULTI-08 (see .planning/REQUIREMENTS.md Future Requirements section)

value: Phase 11 UAT APPROVED 2026-04-30. All steps passed. HEAD: 87b6913 on master. Final findings: (1) messageID must start with 'msg' prefix — UUID rejected. (2) providerID must match configured server provider. (3) Guard fires correctly when AGENTS.md exists (no force). (4) force:true bypasses guard confirmed. (5) Model-driven: no write on empty/sparse dirs even with force — expected behavior. Phase 11 complete.

Phase 5 (Directory Infrastructure) design decisions: (1) Uniform directory schema on all 18 tools — only pass to SDK where endpoint accepts it, document which tools honor it. Consistent tool surface > inconsistent schema. (2) resolveDirectory() ends at undefined NOT process.cwd() — only send directory when explicitly provided via per-tool param or OPENCODE_DEFAULT_PROJECT. Sending process.cwd() unconditionally silently overrides OpenCode session-level directory tracking.
